app.failedHint: Run the report from the project root so report.db is copied to UI/public, then refresh. Or copy report.db to UI/public/report.db manually.

reportSelector.titleReportHistory: Run more reports with preserve_crawl_history to see history here
reportSelector.titleCompareBaseline: Baseline report for URL fingerprint diff (new / removed / changed pages)

common.tableSwipeHint: Swipe sideways to see all columns.

fcp_ms.label: First Contentful Paint
fcp_ms.desc: Time until first text/image is painted. Aim for under 1.8 s.

lcp_ms.label: Largest Contentful Paint
lcp_ms.desc: Time for the largest visible element to render. Core Web Vital. Target ≤2.5 s.

tbt_ms.label: Total Blocking Time
tbt_ms.desc: Total time main thread was blocked during load. Target ≤200 ms.

cls.label: Cumulative Layout Shift
cls.desc: Measures visual stability — unexpected layout shifts. Target ≤0.1.

speed_index_ms.label: Speed Index
speed_index_ms.desc: How quickly content visually appears during load. Target ≤3.4 s.

quickWins.title: Preload LCP image
quickWins.why: Preloading the LCP image resource reduces render-blocking, shaving hundreds of ms from LCP.
quickWins.how: Add <link rel="preload" as="image" href="..."> in <head> for the above-the-fold hero image.

quickWins.title: Add width/height to images
quickWins.why: Missing dimensions cause the browser to reflow layout when images load, increasing CLS.
quickWins.how: Set explicit width and height attributes on all elements.

quickWins.title: Defer non-critical JavaScript
quickWins.why: Render-blocking scripts delay FCP and LCP. Deferring them frees the main thread.
quickWins.how: Add defer or async attribute to <script> tags not needed for initial render.

quickWins.title: Compress images to WebP/AVIF
quickWins.why: Modern formats are 25–50% smaller than JPEG/PNG with equal quality, reducing bytes downloaded.
quickWins.how: Convert images to WebP or AVIF using <picture> with srcset, or serve via CDN with format negotiation.
quickWins.impact: Medium — faster LCP and lower byte count

quickWins.title: Set Cache-Control headers
quickWins.why: Properly cached resources are served from disk on repeat visits, vastly reducing load time.
quickWins.how: Set Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable for hashed assets; use short TTLs for HTML.
quickWins.impact: Medium — major speedup for repeat visitors

quickWins.title: Add meta description
quickWins.why: Meta descriptions improve click-through rate from search results and are used by Lighthouse SEO.
quickWins.how: Add <meta name="description" content="..."> (70–160 chars) to every page.

quickWins.title: Enable text compression
quickWins.why: Gzip or Brotli can reduce HTML/CSS/JS byte sizes by 60–80%, speeding up downloads.
quickWins.how: Enable Gzip or Brotli compression on your server / CDN for text-based content types.
quickWins.impact: High — directly reduces FCP and LCP

quickWins.title: Reduce unused JavaScript
quickWins.why: Unused code wastes bandwidth and parsing time, increasing TBT and FCP.
quickWins.how: Use tree-shaking in your bundler; split large bundles with dynamic import(); remove dead code.

quickWins.title: Minify CSS
quickWins.why: Unminified CSS wastes bytes and can be render-blocking.
quickWins.how: Use a CSS minifier (e.g. cssnano, lightningcss) in your build pipeline.

quickWins.title: Enable HSTS
quickWins.why: HSTS forces HTTPS connections and earns a Best Practices point in Lighthouse.
quickWins.how: Add Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains to your server config.

contentRecommendations.missing_h1: Add exactly one H1 per page.
contentRecommendations.multiple_h1: Use a single H1 per page.
contentRecommendations.thin_content: Expand content to at least 300 characters.

seoIssueRecommendations.h1_missing: Add exactly one H1 per page.
seoIssueRecommendations.h1_multi: Use a single H1 per page.
seoIssueRecommendations.thin_content: Expand content to at least 300 characters.

issues.title: Site Audit
issues.subtitlePrefix: Prioritized issues impacting crawlability, indexing, and rankings.
issues.issuesByCategoryHint: Reflects current search and category filter, not priority filter.
issues.issuesByPriorityHint: Same slice as category chart (search + category filter only).
issues.noMatches: No issues match the current filters.

redirects.title: Redirects
redirects.subtitle: URLs that redirect to another location. From → To.
redirects.chartHint: How many redirect responses use each HTTP status

techStack.title: Tech Detection
techStack.subtitle: Technologies detected across {count} analyzed pages.
techStack.cardHint: Number of pages where each technology was found
techStack.noData: No technology data. Run a crawl first.

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.subtitle: Images discovered during the crawl: on-page assets, Open Graph, and Twitter images. Click any tile to enlarge.
gallery.helpBlurb: Crawled pages with page_analysis.image_urls populate most tiles. OG and Twitter images are merged in when present. Use the header search to filter by page URL or image URL.
gallery.emptyNoImages: No images found. Re-run the crawler with page analysis enabled so image URLs are captured per page.
gallery.emptyNoMatch: No images match your filters or search.

network.title: Internal Linking Map
network.subtitle: Physics-based graph of internal link structure. Red nodes indicate 4xx/5xx errors.

charts.title: Crawl Analytics
charts.subtitle: Status codes, content types, outlinks, title length, and domain distribution.
charts.bubbleTitle: PageRank vs Inlinks vs Word Count
charts.bubbleAria: Bubble chart: inlinks vs word count
charts.scatterAria: Scatter chart: word count vs response time
charts.ariaOutlinks: Bar chart: outlinks distribution by bucket.
charts.ariaTitleLen: Bar chart: title length distribution by character bucket.

content.title: On-Page SEO
content.subtitlePrefix: Audit missing or duplicate titles, meta descriptions, and H1 tags.

filters.label: Missing H1
filters.guidance: Every page should have exactly one H1 tag that clearly describes the page content for both users and search engines.

filters.label: Missing Title
filters.guidance: The <title> tag is a critical ranking signal. Missing titles result in poor click-through rates from search results.

filters.label: Multiple H1s
filters.guidance: Having more than one H1 dilutes your heading hierarchy. Consolidate to a single, keyword-rich H1 per page.

filters.label: Missing Meta Desc
filters.guidance: Meta descriptions influence click-through rates. Write a unique, compelling 120–160 character summary for each page.

filters.label: Meta Desc Short
filters.guidance: Meta descriptions under ~120 characters leave wasted SERP real estate. Expand them to be more descriptive and persuasive.

filters.label: Meta Desc Long
filters.guidance: Meta descriptions over ~160 characters get truncated in search results. Trim them down to the most impactful text.

filters.label: Thin Content
filters.guidance: Pages with very little text (under ~300 words) are often low-value to search engines. Expand or consolidate thin pages.

content.issuesByTypeHint: URL count per on-page issue category
content.emptyFilter: No URLs affected by this issue.
content.emptyGreat: Great job — nothing to fix here.
content.issueTableHint: Rows are pages that match the selected issue. Swipe sideways on small screens if columns don't all fit.

links.title: Link Explorer
links.explorerHint: Each row is a page from this crawl. Scan by title and path; "Links in" counts internal links pointing here (in this crawl). Sort columns or open Inspect for full details.
links.chartWcHint: Same buckets as the word-count filter
links.noUrlData: No data found for this URL.

security.title: Security & Headers
security.subtitlePrefix: HTTP security headers, injection risk, open redirect, and vulnerability findings.
security.findingsByTypeHint: Grouped by finding_type from the scanner
security.emptyFiltered: No findings match the current filters or search.
security.emptyNoScan: Run a crawl with security scanning enabled to see results here.

lighthouse.emptySubtitle: Core Web Vitals and performance audit results.
lighthouse.emptyBodyAfter: and regenerate the report to see results here.
lighthouse.categoriesFallback: Performance, Accessibility, Best practices, SEO, PWA
lighthouse.multiCompareHint: Click any row to view its detailed breakdown below. Sort by any column.
lighthouse.metricsHint: Hover any metric for threshold details. Bars fill relative to the good threshold. Medians from {runs} run(s).
lighthouse.quickWinsHint: Click any card to see why it matters, how to fix it, and the estimated impact.
lighthouse.auditTablesHint: Expand any row for full Lighthouse detail rows (thumbnails, resource URLs, DOM nodes).
lighthouse.diagnosticsHint: Issues grouped by impact area. Click a group to expand. Click any issue for full detail and evidence.
lighthouse.allChecksPassed: No failing audits — all checks passed.

overview.reportComparisonHint: Differences vs the baseline report selected in the header. Uses compact fingerprints (title, meta, headings, word count, content length) — not a full HTML diff. New/removed URLs and pages whose primary text fingerprint changed between runs.
overview.keywordOpportunitiesHint: On-site discovery from crawl copy (titles, headings, meta, slugs). Scores are heuristic — not Google search volume or difficulty.
overview.insightsHint: Distributions and coverage derived from this crawl: where content sits by length, how fast URLs responded, how deep the crawler reached, on-page metadata health, share-preview tags, and—when a Lighthouse run is attached—category scores.
overview.contentDepthHint: 2xx HTML pages by word-count bucket. Heavy left skew often means thin or template-heavy indexable URLs.
overview.serverLatencyHint: All crawled URLs by response time. Tail in slower buckets flags pages to optimize or investigate.
overview.crawlDepthHint: How discovered URLs spread by hop count. A sharp drop after depth 1–2 is common; spikes at high depth may mean long chains or deep archives.
overview.titleMetaHint: Per-page checks across the crawl: missing, length outside recommended ranges, or within target bands (titles ~30–60 chars, descriptions ~70–160).
overview.socialPreviewHint: Share of 2xx HTML pages with Open Graph / Twitter signals. Low og:image hurts link previews on social and chat apps.
overview.readingLevelHint: Grade-level style buckets on 2xx pages. Use to sanity-check whether copy matches your audience.
overview.topMimeHint: What the crawler actually fetched. Large non-HTML counts can mean assets, feeds, or mis-linked URLs in scope.
overview.lhCategoryHint: From the attached Lighthouse summary (typically the audited landing or representative URL). Scores are 0–100; bars use the same good / needs work / poor coloring as elsewhere.
overview.topPagesHint: From your crawl's internal link graph: importance is a PageRank-style score (relative to this site, not Google). Connections = edges touching this URL in the graph.
overview.noTopSearch: No top pages match your search.
overview.noTopPagesData: No top pages data (crawl had no edges or outlinks).
overview.groupedTitleMetaAria: Grouped comparison of title tag and meta description length health across all crawled pages.
overview.ariaWordCountIntro: Word count buckets for successful HTML pages:
overview.ariaDepthIntro: URLs by crawl depth from start URL:
overview.ariaSocialIntro: Social preview coverage on HTML pages:

contentAnalytics.title: Content Insights
contentAnalytics.subtitle: Word count, readability, content-to-HTML ratio, top keywords, social meta coverage, crawl health, and on-page flags.
contentAnalytics.noThinSearch: No thin pages match your search.
contentAnalytics.socialAllGood: All pages have social meta tags, or no data available yet. Run a crawl to populate.

sqlPlayground.title: Audit database
sqlPlayground.subtitle: Explore the crawl SQLite file like a small SQL lab: schema browser, guided examples, syntax-highlighted editor, and results below. Everything runs in your browser.
sqlPlayground.panelEmpty: No user tables in this file.
sqlPlayground.schemaTruncated: List truncated. Query sqlite_master for the full list.
sqlPlayground.localLoadError: Could not read that file as SQLite.
sqlPlayground.resultsEmptyHint: Results from SELECT statements appear here.
sqlPlayground.resultsExplorerHint: Sticky header · drag column edges to resize · double-click a handle to reset widths · hover cells for full text.
sqlPlayground.resetColumnWidthsHint: Double-click to reset all column widths
sqlPlayground.resultsUiTruncated: Only the first {shown} rows are shown here ({total} returned). Add LIMIT to your query for a smaller grid.
sqlPlayground.examplesEmpty: No examples match this database or filters.

modelLoader.title: LLM lab
modelLoader.subtitle: Chat with your chosen model. Open Settings to pick provider, model name, API keys, and advanced tools.

browser.intro: Runs entirely in your browser via Transformers.js. Use ONNX-compatible Hub models (often the Xenova organization).
browser.hfModelsLink: Browse Transformers.js–compatible models on Hugging Face
browser.readyHint: Pipeline ready. You can run a smoke test or switch models.
browser.onnxHint: If this persists, try another model, clear site data, or reload. Large models can exhaust WASM memory.

api.intro: Send a test prompt using your API key. Keys are not sent to this app’s servers—requests go from your browser to the provider.
api.keyWarning: Never share API keys or commit them to git. For production, use a backend proxy instead of exposing keys in the browser.
api.corsWarning: Some providers block browser requests (CORS). If a request fails with a network or CORS error, use Copy curl and run the command in your terminal, or use a proxy.
api.rememberSession: Remember keys for this browser tab session (sessionStorage)
api.corsDetail: This is often a CORS restriction: the provider does not allow browser origins to call the API directly. Use Copy curl to run the same request from your machine, or route traffic through a server you control.

chat.intro: A ChatGPT-style thread: multi-turn conversation with Google Gemini or an OpenAI-compatible API. Uses the same API key and model fields as the API tab.
chat.systemPlaceholder: e.g. You are a concise coding assistant.
chat.emptyHint: Send a message to start the conversation.
chat.openSettingsHint: Use Settings (top right) to choose Gemini or OpenAI, set the model name, and paste your API key.
chat.imageMarkdownNotice: Markdown in replies can include images from HTTPS URLs. Attach images here to send them to vision-capable models.
chat.footerNote: Responses are generated by the model and may be inaccurate. Verify important facts.
chat.curlModeHint: Curl mode: each Send merges the chat thread into your JSON body. OpenAI-style: messages[] is replaced with system (your Settings text + optional DB schema) plus the conversation. Gemini generateContent: contents[] is filled and systemInstruction is set or merged with your template. For manual curl in a terminal, put the schema in the system message: OpenAI: messages[0] = { role: "system", content: "<paste schema>" }; Gemini: "systemInstruction": { "parts": [{ "text": "<paste schema>" }] }. Use Copy schema below to grab the block.
chat.curlChatUrlRequired: Set a request URL in Settings → Curl before sending.
chat.curlChatBodyInvalid: Curl body must be valid JSON for chat. Use an OpenAI-compatible object (e.g. include model and any other fields your API needs); messages will be filled automatically.
chat.curlChatMethodPost: Chat over Curl requires POST, PUT, or PATCH so a JSON body can be sent.
chat.dbSettingsHint: When the audit DB is loaded, schema is added to the model context. Tool calling (executing read-only SQL, etc.) runs only if you choose Google Gemini or OpenAI in the lab tool dropdown—not Curl. Curl mode still merges schema into system / systemInstruction for each Send.
chat.curlToolsExplainer: With a loaded audit DB: Curl + Gemini uses the same REST tool loop and `generativelanguage.googleapis.com` as your template (model from Settings). Curl + OpenAI uses your Settings base URL and key. Put your API key in Settings or in Curl headers (X-goog-api-key / Authorization: Bearer).
chat.curlToolsGeminiKeyRequired: For tools with Curl: add your Gemini API key under Settings → Model & API, or X-goog-api-key in the Curl tab headers.
chat.curlToolsOpenAiKeyRequired: For tools with Curl: add your OpenAI API key under Settings → Model & API, or Authorization: Bearer … in the Curl headers.
chat.curlDbToolsHint: A database is loaded: each Send appends the schema to system instructions (OpenAI system message or Gemini systemInstruction). If your curl body already defines systemInstruction, the app merges the schema after your text. For a shell curl, use Copy schema and paste into system content as shown in the curl hint above.
chat.dbCopySchemaTitle: Copy markdown + JSON schema for pasting into a manual curl request body

curl.intro: Paste a curl command to fill the fields below. Parsing is best-effort (quotes and common flags). Then edit and Send—same CORS limits apply as in the API tab. Use “Load Gemini REST template” for `generativelanguage.googleapis.com/.../models/MODEL:generateContent` with `X-goog-api-key`, or “Load OpenAI Chat template” for `…/v1/chat/completions` with `Authorization: Bearer`.
curl.geminiTemplateHint: Sets URL, POST, headers, and a sample multi-turn `contents` array with `generationConfig.responseMimeType` for JSON. If the body is `{}` or missing `contents`, Send fills a placeholder turn. Chat with Curl transport still merges the full thread from the composer.
curl.openaiTemplateHint: Sets URL to your Settings base + `/chat/completions`, POST, optional Bearer header, and a sample `messages` array with `response_format` JSON mode and `temperature`.
curl.corsHint: If you see a CORS or network error, the target host may forbid browser calls. Run the equivalent curl in a terminal or use a proxy.

similarPages.title: Similar pages (TF-IDF)
similarPages.errNotEnoughText: Not enough text on this page to embed (add title/H1/description).
similarPages.errNoEmbedding: Could not compute embedding for this page.

pageAnalysis.previewFailed: Preview failed to load (hotlink or CORS). Open link below.
pageAnalysis.similarInternalCaption: Precomputed similar internal URLs (cosine vs site pages)
pageAnalysis.failingAuditsCaption: Failing audits — expand for tables, thumbnails, and DOM nodes ({count})
pageAnalysis.noLhData: No Lighthouse data for this URL. Run a crawl with per-page Lighthouse enabled to see Core Web Vitals and performance recommendations here.
pageAnalysis.noMatchingWarnings: No matching warnings for this page.

content.intro: Content metrics for this URL, with the same chart styles as Content Insights. Site-wide charts help you see where this page sits in the crawl.
content.vsSiteDesc: Compare word count to crawl-wide mean and median.
content.textVsMarkupDesc: Share of visible text in the HTML document
content.siteDistDesc: Same buckets as Content Insights. Caption shows where this page lands.
content.notEnoughReading: Not enough text for a reading level on this page.
content.noKeywordPage: No keyword data for this page.
content.onPageSignalsDesc: Title length, meta description, and H1 count — same categories as Content Insights.
content.socialMetaDesc: Open Graph, Twitter Card, and OG image presence on this URL.
content.siteArchDesc: Crawl depth across the site. This page is at the depth shown in the KPI row above.

securityRows.label: Strict-Transport-Security
securityRows.rec: Set HSTS with max-age of at least 31536000 to enforce HTTPS.

securityRows.label: X-Content-Type-Options
securityRows.rec: Set to "nosniff" to prevent MIME-type sniffing attacks.

securityRows.label: X-Frame-Options
securityRows.rec: Set to "SAMEORIGIN" or "DENY" to prevent clickjacking.

securityRows.label: Content-Security-Policy
securityRows.rec: Define a strict CSP to reduce XSS attack surface.

issues.issuesBySource: Issues by source for this URL

browserMlChat.welcome: Ask questions about this audit. The model runs in your browser; first load may download model files.
browserMlChat.chatSystemPrompt: You are a concise technical assistant helping the user understand an SEO / site crawl audit. Prefer short answers. When citing data, refer to the report sections provided. If the user asks for something not in the report, say you do not have that data.
browserMlChat.chatErrorGeneric: Something went wrong while generating a reply.
browserMlChat.chatErrorRangeBuffer: The in-browser model hit a memory or WASM limit. Try reloading the page, clearing site data, or using a smaller model.
browserMlChat.toolUnknownCommand: Unknown command. Type /help for available commands.
browserMlChat.chatPlaceholder: Message… (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline)
browserMlChat.systemPromptHint: Instructions for the in-browser model. Tool usage rules and your audit data are still appended automatically after this text.
